text:
As a sunbeam bounces against a dewdrop.

There's a small fairy whose favorite thing to do is watch sunbeams. It was especially delightful today when one bounced right into a little puddle. She flew over to admire it.

"What a bright little thing!" she praised.

She watched it sparkle for a while when something peculiar happened—a tiny shadow emerged and the sunbeam dimmed. The fairy frowned.

"Why so gloomy?" she asked.

"Oh," the shadow said. "Today's the day I pretend to become a giant rock."

The fairy tilted her head.

"Really?" She smiled mysteriously.

The shadow thought for a moment.

"Yes! At least then I don't get to shine for anyone."

She giggled to herself. "You'll never be a rock. Sunbeams love company."

With that encouragement, the shadow let go and danced around in the light again. The fairy watched in delight.

"Nature's quite the comedian," she said.
